one_time_description: Gain from investments in First Nation business entities (primarily Clarke Lake Geothermal Limited Partnership development via Deh Tai Limited Partnership)
economic_notes: Deh Tai Limited Partnership holds investments in road construction (Eh Cho Dene LP), commercial rental properties (Fern Lake Commercial Holdings LP), residential rental properties (Fern Lake Residential Holdings LP), and geothermal electricity production (Clarke Lake Geothermal LP). Clarke Lake capitalized $16,721,727 in development costs for a geothermal project during the year. FNFN Forestry Limited Partnership operates forestry operations near Fort Nelson. Liard Hotsprings Lodge provides accommodations, restaurant and gasoline/souvenir sales. 1164062 B.C. Ltd. provides hotel and restaurant accommodations in Fort Nelson.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2022. Large one-time gain of $14,955,565 from investments in First Nation business entities (primarily Deh Tai Limited Partnership via Clarke Lake Geothermal LP) drove the large surplus. The auditor firm name is listed only as 'Chartered Professional Accountants' from Surrey, BC — no firm name explicitly stated in the extracted text. Consolidated entities include Liard Hotsprings Lodge (FNFN) Ltd., Chalo Independent School Society, Fort Nelson Indian Band Land Trust Society, and Reaching For Our Vision Trust. Three entities (Deh Tai GP Ltd., Deh Tai Limited Partnership, FNFN Forestry Limited Partnership) are accounted for under the modified equity method. Waterways Communications Limited Partnership (51%) also accounted for under modified equity. Business entity financials are as at December 31, 2021 (different year-end). Restricted cash of $505,775 included in cash. Designated funds of $2,914,073 also included in cash. Funds held in trust (Ottawa Trust) total $9,915,971. Tangible capital asset net book value not explicitly stated on face of balance sheet in the extracted text. Prior year accumulated surplus was $58,976,246. A specific land claim is before BC Supreme Court under Treaty 8; promissory notes payable to Canada exist. CMHC mortgage advance of $14,999 received during year.
